title: When Predicting Trends: - Ask Yourself:
subtitle: Expect the Unexpected
description:
When predicting trends:
- Ask yourself: "Could there be sudden changes or breaks in this trend?"
- Prepare for shifts: Always consider outliers and unexpected events in planning.
- Look for weak signals: Early signs of change can help you adapt quickly.

Example: A stock steadily rising? Plan for a downturn just as much as for continued growth.

faq.answer: Don't worry! Life OS habits are designed to be flexible. Just get back on track the next day without judgment.

howTo.title: Define the metric baseline
howTo.summary: Identify the trend you want to analyze. Specify the metric, time window, and current slope to establish a clear baseline for your observations.

howTo.title: List potential failure modes
howTo.summary: Ask yourself how the trend could suddenly change. Identify at least three potential failure modes, considering external shocks, internal dependencies, and measurement errors.

howTo.title: Choose a diagnostic or hedge
howTo.summary: Select a small, actionable hedge or diagnostic that costs 5% or less of your resources. This could involve monitoring a specific signal or adjusting your strategy.

howTo.title: Run a weak-signal scan
howTo.summary: Conduct a 15-minute scan to identify low-volume clues that may indicate a trend shift. Look for anomalies in your data and prioritize them for action.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your findings and actions in the Brali LifeOS app to track your progress and maintain the habit of monitoring trends. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/anticipate-sudden-trend-shifts/.
